Da tifoso non accetto la mediocritГ  e non la accetterГІ mai passivamente. Il Milan non puГІ essere ridotto a вҖңsoluzione meno peggioвҖқ, a minestrina, a quarto posto come orizzonte massimo, a progetto da raccontare ogni estate dopo aver distrutto quello precedente.

PerГІ ormai la sensazione ГЁ che ci stiano prendendo per sfinimento. Tre settimane di nulla, casting infiniti, nomi buttati lГ¬, incontri, attese, deadline spostate. Alla fine arrivi quasi a dire: va bene, fate almeno qualcosa. Ed ГЁ questo il punto piГ№ triste.

Rangnick-Glasner puГІ anche essere meglio del vuoto totale, ma non deve diventare lвҖҷalibi per assolvere chi ha portato il Milan a questo livello.

Io spero solo che questa proprietГ  se ne vada prima di fare altri danni. PerchГ© ogni errore in piГ№, ogni giocatore svalutato, ogni talento perso, ogni stagione buttata, rallenterГ  anche il percorso di crescita di unвҖҷeventuale nuova societГ .

Il problema non ГЁ solo oggi. ГҲ il Milan che rischiamo di ritrovarci domani, pieno di macerie da ricostruire.

comments.author: @JTRave
comments.text:
Biggest 3 moments for me:

1) Cape Verde 0-0 Spain. Biggest upset of the tournament thatвҖҷs already had a lot of shocks; for the odds-on favorite to fail to win against a debutant nation with a population of 500k is an incredible achievement for the underdogs. Their World Cup is, frankly, already a success and everything from here is just icing.

2) USA 4-1 Paraguay. Yes, perhaps a little bit of bias, but it must be understood that this isnвҖҷt just a strong win. ItвҖҷs literally Team USAвҖҷs greatest game of football theyвҖҷve ever played in terms of skill. For many Americans, weвҖҷve been promised our whole lives that the USA was always on the precipice of reaching вҖңthe next levelвҖқ, but then weвҖҷre still losing in the same spots, and even when we win itвҖҷs by slim margins and on the backs of scrappy play. This was different. This was Team USA being far and away the вҖңbetter teamвҖқ in a way that we typically only see from the best European and South American sides. So in that sense, this match was truly historic. ItвҖҷs an indication that this вҖңGolden GenerationвҖқ may not be squandered, and there is legitimate hope for a deep run.

3) Mbappe and Messi. Last tournamentвҖҷs superstar finalists. Each has a World Cup already to his name. One of them is the greatest player in the world. The other is the greatest player of all time. And they both showed up Matchday 1 and BROUGHT IT. Mbappe with two goals against a quality Senegal side, his second goal seemed like it was out of pure spite. Messi with a hat trick that shows the full diversity of his skillset at 38 years old. They both seem destined to not just break but shatter KloseвҖҷs career World Cup goals record. And it is so nice to see these stars rise to the occasion and prove to the world that even at the very top level, thereвҖҷs a difference between вҖңworld classвҖқ and something greater.

comments.author: @FreddysCat
comments.text:
Just wanted to see a competitive match, but it was more Coppa di Merda. English announcers sounding more and more like the UK's hyperbolic American masters. ... Every player name preceded by the name of the foreign colony ("club") he plays for in the UK. ... 2 goals in. Bored. Back to Verona. More authenticity and insight into the sport and life generally--for better and worse--on one page than anything that will happen in El Norte. Infantino guastafeste.

'A journalist called me up from Gazzetta dello Sport to say he was preparing a book on the great teams of the last thirty years and had I followed Hellas Verona in the miraculous 1985. I had to admit, not without an old chagrin, that I hadnвҖҷt. I hadnвҖҷt had a son as an excuse at the time. I couldnвҖҷt plead family matters and male bonding.

'To avoid the memory of one of lifeвҖҷs great missed opportunities, I ask him, вҖҳWhat do you think of TrapattoniвҖҷs Italy?вҖҷ The national team has a new coach. вҖҳCouldnвҖҷt care less,вҖҷ the sports journalist says. вҖҳIвҖҷm a Genoa fan.вҖҷ '

No reason for a Christian to value someone more because a grandparent (FIFA's standard) came from a particular place on the map. To care about nations seems hidebound anyway as they become more and more abstracted. (This is why the right are so rabidly insisting on them today. And, of course, mine is better than yours.) Globalism began taking shape during the Age of Exploration 500 years ago, so don't go on too much about your latest conspiracy while at the same time buying oranges during the bleak mid-winter.

club > country

We should keep (& start) Nwaneri RW next season as Saka will need to play much less next season, and along with Rice will need a few weeks break at least after the WC is over.

comments.author: @jlb74a
comments.text:
RB Freeman has been a positive surprise.
It was his fault though on the Turkish third goal.

As right back, heвҖҷs responsible for marking the player at the far post, instead he wanted to help and mark other players in front of the goal, thatвҖҷs the job of the CBs midfielders.

ThatвҖҷs exactly what happened on the cross. The Turkish player, all alone was able to stop the ball, bring it down and shoot pass Turner. The ball was partially blocked by Freeman at the goal line, the ball landed on a Turkish forward for a tap in.

Not long ago, Freeman was a forward, then converted to a midfielder, now he is a converted defender.

He must learn to complete his own assignments first, then he can help out others.

IвҖҷm sure he will keep learning valuable lessons with each loss.

comments.author: @TheJTell93
comments.text: Offside, Irans number 4 toe is just across the second to last defenders heel. There needs to be two defending players in front of the attacking player at the moment the ball is passed/shot. In this case, if you take a look at the high angle replay and pause just at the moment Irans 21 shot at goal, the keeper which is usually the last defender is out, which makes egypts number 2 on the top right last defender. Egypts Number nines heel being slightly further back than the goalies whole body makes him the second to last player. Therefore this call is valid

Morocco is simply the best team in Africa. рҹҮІрҹҮҰрҹ”Ҙ Their football is on another levelвҖ”right now, nobody in Africa is matching them.

Algeria... what happened? рҹҳӯ That performance looked like the controller disconnected.

I still remember some players from Senegal and Algeria talking about winning the World Cup, while Morocco never even talked that muchвҖ”they just stayed humble and focused. Morocco let their football do the talking instead of making big promises.

That's the difference. Some teams celebrate interviews and podcasts. Morocco celebrates results. Stay humble, respect your opponents, and let the pitch do the talking. That's why Morocco keeps making history while others keep talking about what they're going to do. рҹҳӮрҹҮІрҹҮҰ
